summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a stellar May 23 1972 second set, highlighted by the definitive Dark Star → Morning Dew sequence.
review: The recording is a pristine stereo sound‑board mix that fans describe as crisp and hi‑fi. Musically the show shines with a legendary Dark Star flowing into an ethereal Morning Dew, plus standout performances of He's Gone, Sugar Magnolia, Comes A Time and what many consider the best version of Two Souls in Communion. Reviewers are uniformly enthusiastic, calling it one of the better second sets of that tour.

ai_rating.rationale: All five reviews give 5‑star ratings and praise both the musical performance (especially Dark Star→Morning Dew) and the high‑quality soundboard mix.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Stereo hi‑fi sound with an excellent mix; listeners note crystal‑clear clarity and balanced instrument separation.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Partial second‑set recording that flows seamlessly from the soaring Dark Star into a gentle Morning Dew, followed by solid renditions of He's Gone, Sugar Magnolia and Comes A Time; reviewers cite it as one of the stronger second sets of the spring ’72 tour.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His guitar accents and tone are repeatedly praised, with reviewers calling him one of rock's greatest lead‑guitarists.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass tone is highlighted as exceptional during the Dark Star/Morning Dew sequence.
